request: Rank the 4 best Audi Q7 generations overall across the full production run. Use 4 different generations when available, with one representative version from each generation. If the model has fewer than 4 true generations, use the most important era-defining versions across its history.
title: RANKING THE BEST AUDI Q7 GENERATIONS
close_narration: So, which Q7 generation would you park in your driveway?
order_rationale: Ordered from the earliest to the latest generation, highlighting the evolution and key features of each.

entries.name: First Generation Q7 4.2 FSI
entries.label: PIONEERING LUXURY SUV
entries.one_line_fact: Debuting in 2005 at $50,000, the first-gen Q7's 4.2 FSI V8 delivered 350 horsepower, setting the stage for Audi's luxury SUV dominance. Today, clean examples trade around $12K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $12K.
entries.narration: Debuting in 2005 at $50,000, the first-gen Q7's 4.2 FSI V8 delivered 350 horsepower, setting the stage for Audi's luxury SUV dominance. Today, clean examples trade around $12K.

entries.name: First Generation Q7 V12 TDI
entries.label: DIESEL POWERHOUSE
entries.one_line_fact: In 2008, the Q7 V12 TDI roared onto the scene with 500 horsepower and a $130,000 price tag, boasting a monstrous 6.0-liter diesel engine. Today, clean examples trade around $10K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $10K.
entries.narration: In 2008, the Q7 V12 TDI roared onto the scene with 500 horsepower and a $130,000 price tag, boasting a monstrous 6.0-liter diesel engine. Today, clean examples trade around $10K.

entries.name: Second Generation Q7 3.0 TFSI
entries.label: LIGHTER AND AGILE
entries.one_line_fact: The 2015 Q7 3.0 TFSI, starting at $55,000, shed over 700 pounds, offering 333 horsepower and enhanced agility. Today, clean examples trade around $18K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $18K.
entries.narration: The 2015 Q7 3.0 TFSI, starting at $55,000, shed over 700 pounds, offering 333 horsepower and enhanced agility. Today, clean examples trade around $18K.

entries.name: Second Generation Q7 e-tron
entries.label: HYBRID INNOVATOR
entries.one_line_fact: Launched in 2016 at $80,000, the Q7 e-tron combined a diesel engine with electric power, delivering 373 horsepower and eco-friendly luxury. Today, clean examples trade around $17K.
entries.current_value_note: Recent Cars & Bids examples trade around $17K.
entries.narration: Launched in 2016 at $80,000, the Q7 e-tron combined a diesel engine with electric power, delivering 373 horsepower and eco-friendly luxury. Today, clean examples trade around $17K.
